metadata.title: Why an Accurate Model Can Still Lie Every Time It Says 99%
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Computational Skepticism for AI candidate 10. Source chapter: books/computational-skepticism-for-ai/chapters/02-probability-uncertainty-and-the-confidence-illusion.md (calibration section only).
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = calibrated region / diagonal / honest zone; CRIMSON = overconfident tail / peel / overclaiming
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no ECE or Brier formulas, no temperature-scaling or softmax mechanics, no human-forecaster calibration tangent — one model, one curve
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B16: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Why an accurate model can still lie about how sure it is.

card.copy:
Why an Accurate Model Can Still
Lie Every Time It Says 99%

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. Why an accurate model can still lie about how sure it is.

beats.narration_text: The vendor hands you a number: ninety-four percent accurate. Every metric on the dashboard is green. You sign off.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The vendor hands you a number: ninety-four percent accurate. Every metric on the dashboard is green. You sign off.

beats.narration_text: When the model says ninety-nine percent confident — does that actually happen ninety-nine times out of a hundred?

props.headline: When the model says ninety-nine percent confident — does that actually…

card.copy: When the model says ninety-nine percent confident — does that actually happen ninety-nine times out of a hundred?

beats.narration_text: Accuracy counts correct labels. Confidence is a separate claim — how certain the model is, not just whether it got the label right.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Accuracy counts correct labels. Confidence is a separate claim — how certain the model is, not just whether it got the label right.

beats.narration_text: When the model assigns ninety-nine percent to a case, it is making a promise: ninety-nine of every hundred similar cases should be positive.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: When the model assigns ninety-nine percent to a case, it is making a promise: ninety-nine of every hundred similar cases should be positive.

beats.narration_text: Run the model on a held-out set. Collect every case where it reported ninety-nine percent confidence. Then check what actually happened.

props.headline: Run the model on a held-out set. Collect every case where…

beats.image_description: A stream of prediction outputs on a screen, each labeled with a high confidence percentage, against a blurred data-center background

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Run the model on a held-out set. Collect every case where it reported ninety-nine percent confidence. Then check what actually happened.

beats.narration_text: There is a diagnostic for this. Stated confidence on one axis, actual frequency of positives on the other. A perfectly calibrated model traces the diagonal.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: There is a diagnostic for this. Stated confidence on one axis, actual frequency of positives on the other. A perfectly calibrated model traces the diagonal.

beats.narration_text: Sort every prediction into a confidence bin. One bin for the low-confidence calls, one for the medium, one for the high. Each bin collects its cases.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Sort every prediction into a confidence bin. One bin for the low-confidence calls, one for the medium, one for the high. Each bin collects its cases.

beats.narration_text: If the model says seventy percent confident on a thousand cases, you want roughly seven hundred of those cases to turn out positive.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: If the model says seventy percent confident on a thousand cases, you want roughly seven hundred of those cases to turn out positive.

beats.narration_text: Count positives in each bin. Low-confidence bins track the claim. High-confidence bins fall short — outcomes arrive well below the stated number.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Count positives in each bin. Low-confidence bins track the claim. High-confidence bins fall short — outcomes arrive well below the stated number.

beats.narration_text: Connect those frequencies. The curve tracks the diagonal through the middle — then peels away at the top, where the model overclaims.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Connect those frequencies. The curve tracks the diagonal through the middle — then peels away at the top, where the model overclaims.

beats.narration_text: A net will say ninety-nine percent when it should say eighty-five, and it will say it fluently.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A net will say ninety-nine percent when it should say eighty-five, and it will say it fluently.

beats.narration_text: A medical risk model assigns ninety-nine percent confidence to four hundred patients. Count the outcomes: three hundred ten of those four hundred actually have the condition. That is seventy-seven percent, not ninety-nine. Every one of those four hundred cases, the model said essentially certain. Reality said less than four in five. The score was precise. It was also wrong.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A medical risk model assigns ninety-nine percent confidence to four hundred patients. Count the outcomes: three hundred ten of those four hundred actually have

beats.narration_text: Accuracy measures correct calls. Calibration measures honest ones.

props.headline: Accuracy measures correct calls. Calibration measures honest ones.
